steps.title: Determine slope and intercept signs from the graph
steps.content:
The line clearly rises from left to right, indicating a positive slope.

The line crosses the y-axis above the origin, indicating a positive y-intercept.

This immediately eliminates choices B (negative slope), C (negative intercept), and D (both negative).

Only choice A has both positive values.

steps.title: Read the y-intercept directly from the graph
steps.content: The line crosses the y-axis at approximately $y = 0.8$, which matches the value in choice A.

steps.title: Calculate the slope using two points on the line
steps.content:
Select two clear points on the line: approximately $(2, 4.2)$ and $(5, 9)$.

$$\text{slope} = \frac{9 - 4.2}{5 - 2} = \frac{4.8}{3} = 1.6 \approx 1.7$$

This slope of approximately $1.7$, combined with the y-intercept of $0.8$, gives us the equation $y = 0.8 + 1.7x$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
